Identifying the correct entry simultaneously resolves the christening date, place, and parentage — making this the gatekeeper question for the entire objective.

entry.jurisdiction: Poulton-le-Fylde and neighboring parishes, Lancashire, England
entry.rationale: England, Lancashire, Parish Registers 1538-1910 (collection 1465701) contains indexed and imaged christening registers for all Lancashire parishes in this period. Searching for 'Robert Swarbrick' c. 1797 will surface both candidate christening entries (the two parishes named in the objective), each recording the father's name. The father's name across the two entries is the primary discriminating evidence. This is the most direct path to the answer and the free-first priority.

Search 'England, Births and Christenings, 1538-1975' (collection 1473014) for Robert Swarbrick and his putative siblings under the candidate parent couples. Identifying other children of the same parent couple in the registers corroborates which family unit Robert belonged to, and whether the Poulton-le-Fylde or neighboring-parish couple's children remained geographically consistent with Robert's later life (marriage at Poulton-le-Fylde 1821, death at Hambleton 1839, burial at St. Chad Poulton-le-Fylde 1839).

Search Lancashire Parish Registers (collection 1465701) for John Swarbrick and Grace Collinson's children at St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde. John Swarbrick (L88T-VT4) married Grace Collinson 28 October 1788 at St. Chad and is noted 'of Carleton, Poulton-le-Fylde.' If their children — including any Robert — are consistently baptised at St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde, that is corroborating evidence that the Poulton-le-Fylde entry is our subject's. If their children appear at a different parish, that shifts the identification.

Search for a marriage allegation or bond for Robert Swarbrick's February 1821 marriage to Ellen Gardner at Poulton-le-Fylde. Pre-1754 and some post-1754 marriage bonds and allegations in the Chester Diocese (which covered Lancashire) often name the groom's father or guardian. If such a document survives and names Robert's father, it provides direct corroborating evidence independent of the christening register. Chester Diocese records are partially indexed on FamilySearch.

Search the Lancashire Online Parish Clerks (lan-opc.org.uk) — a free, volunteer-transcribed index covering many Fylde-area parishes — for Robert Swarbrick christening entries c. 1797. The OPC database may include parishes or date ranges not yet fully indexed on FamilySearch, providing a cross-check on the two candidate entries found in Step 1 and confirming the full text of each entry (e.g. mother's name where only father is indexed on FS).

Search 'Lancashire, England, Church of England Baptisms, Marriages and Burials, 1538-1812' on Ancestry as a paid cross-check if the FamilySearch index (Step 1) returns only one of the two 1797 Robert Swarbrick entries or leaves parent names ambiguous. Ancestry's transcription of the same Lancashire registers may supply details (mother's name, township) omitted from the FamilySearch index.

Then pli_002: search "England, Births and Christenings, 1538-1975" (FS collection 1473014) for Robert Swarbrick 1797 Lancashire and siblings of candidate parent couples 1780-1815. Then pli_003: search Lancashire Parish Registers for John Swarbrick + Grace Collinson children at St. Chad Poulton-le-Fylde 1785-1800. args.notes: Found both 1797 candidate christening entries in collection 1473014 (England, Births and Christenings, 1538-1975): (1) ark:/61903/1:1:V5GJ-FJG — Robert Swarbrick, 11 Jun 1797, Hambleton, father William Swarbrick, mother Ann; (2) ark:/61903/1:1:NX2H-W9B — Robert Swarbrick, 20 Dec 1797, Poulton le Fylde, father John Swarbrick, mother Grace. The Poulton-le-Fylde entry matches John Swarbrick (L88T-VT4) + Grace Collinson (P6MH-1N8) already in tree, who married at St. Chad Poulton-le-Fylde 1788. Subject MM4W-19N married at Poulton-le-Fylde 1821 and was buried there 1839 — strong geographic match to Poulton-le-Fylde entry.

Found 9 christening entries for children of John Swarbrick + Grace, all at Poulton-le-Fylde or Marton (same register, dual-indexed): Alice (8 Mar 1789), Margaret (6 Mar 1791), Betty (17 Feb 1793), Jane (9 Jun 1795), Robert (20 Dec 1797, ark:/61903/1:1:NX2H-W9B), John (20 Dec 1797, ark:/61903/1:1:NX2H-WS9 — twin of Robert). Robert and John were christened the same day, confirming twin birth. The consistent Poulton-le-Fylde christening parish for this sibling cluster strongly corroborates that Robert Swarbrick christened 20 Dec 1797 at Poulton-le-Fylde (son of John Swarbrick and Grace) is MM4W-19N. The competing 11 Jun 1797 Hambleton christening (father William Swarbrick, mother Ann) belongs to a different family.

Search returned christening records of MM4W-19N's children (Robert as father) but no marriage bond or allegation naming his own father. Marriage bonds/allegations were abolished in England by the Marriage Act 1753; no such document exists for an 1821 marriage. The marriage register entry itself (existing tree source Q5X3-71R / ark NX1Z-4QD) does not name parents. Pli_004 approach was misconceived for post-1754 England — no additional parentage evidence obtainable via this route.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Searched Lancashire parish registers via two FamilySearch collections (1465701, 1473014), a family-context sibling cluster search for children of John Swarbrick and Grace, and attempted marriage bonds (inapplicable post-1754 England). Both competing 1797 christening entries were found and distinguished. OPC and Ancestry fallbacks were skipped once both entries and full sibling cluster were located on FamilySearch. All relevant record types for a 1797 Lancashire christening question were consulted; the question is answered with two corroborating sources naming the same parents plus the sibling cluster. Original register images are accessible but were not examined — this limitation is reflected in the proof tier.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes. The research directly answers q_001: Robert Swarbrick of Hambleton/Poulton-le-Fylde (MM4W-19N) was christened 20 December 1797 at Poulton-le-Fylde, Lancashire, son of John Swarbrick (L88T-VT4) and Grace [Collinson] (P6MH-1N8). The competing 11 June 1797 Hambleton entry names William Swarbrick and Ann — a demonstrably different family.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch collections 1465701 (Lancashire Parish Registers) and 1473014 (England, Births and Christenings) searched. Marriage bonds searched (pli_004: inapplicable, abolished 1754). Sibling cluster search (pli_003) confirmed family geography. OPC (pli_005) and Ancestry (pli_006) were planned fallbacks but skipped once the question was answered on FamilySearch; no evidence suggests those repositories would materially change the finding.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: All three sources (src_001–src_003) are derivative FamilySearch indexes. Original register images exist and are accessible (image URL noted in src_002 notes for NX2H-W9B). The images were not examined. The GSU microfilming program is systematic and the index is internally consistent; the sibling cluster provides corroboration across nine separate entries. The derivative evidence is primary-information (minister-recorded at time of event) even if the source layer is derivative. This limitation is the primary reason the proof tier is probable rather than proved.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Two evidence units corroborate John Swarbrick and Grace as parents of the subject Robert: (1) NX2H-W9B — Robert's own christening entry, 20 Dec 1797, Poulton-le-Fylde, father John Swarbrick, mother Grace; (2) the sibling cluster (log_003) — nine children of John Swarbrick and Grace at Poulton-le-Fylde over 1789–1797, derived independently by searching for the parents' children, not for the subject. The twin's entry (NX2H-WS9) is from the same register event and informant as NX2H-W9B and counts as the same information unit, not a separate unit.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Primary information from derivative sources. The christening entries were recorded by the officiating minister at the time of christening (primary information, direct evidence) but are accessed only via FamilySearch derivative indexes, not via the original register images. This satisfies the spirit of GPS Element 3 (primary information) but not full GPS Element 1 (original record examination).
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: The disambiguation between the two Robert Swarbrick christenings is fully resolved. The Hambleton entry (V5GJ-FJG, 11 Jun 1797) names father William Swarbrick and mother Ann — not John Swarbrick and Grace Collinson, who are MM4W-19N's family. MM4W-19N's marriage at Poulton-le-Fylde (1821) and burial at St. Chad Poulton-le-Fylde (1839) are geographically consistent only with the Poulton-le-Fylde christening. No unresolved conflicts remain.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. The only unsearched source capable of changing the conclusion is the original register image for NX2H-W9B. A systematic transcription error that changed both the father's name (from, say, William to John) and the christening parish (from Hambleton to Poulton-le-Fylde) simultaneously would be required to overturn the conclusion — and the sibling cluster (nine separately indexed entries) would also have to be wrong. No evidence suggests any transcription error in the index. Risk is real but low; noting for future image verification.

entry.narrative_markdown:
# Christening and Parents of Robert Swarbrick (c. 1797–1839), Poulton-le-Fylde, Lancashire

## Research Question

Which of the two Lancashire christening entries for "Robert Swarbrick" recorded in 1797 belongs to Robert Swarbrick of Hambleton (died c. 20 November 1839, buried 22 November 1839 at St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde), and who were his parents?

## Conclusion

The evidence strongly suggests that Robert Swarbrick (FamilySearch ID MM4W-19N) was christened 20 December 1797 at St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde, Lancashire, England, as the son of John Swarbrick (born c. 1764, buried St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde, 7 August 1830) and Grace Collinson his wife (christened 17 July 1768 at Poulton-le-Fylde, buried there 25 April 1825), who had married at St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde, on 28 October 1788. A competing 1797 Lancashire christening entry — Robert Swarbrick, 11 June 1797, Hambleton, father William Swarbrick, mother Ann — belongs to a different, same-named individual and is not the record of the subject.

## Evidence

### Robert's Christening Entry

A FamilySearch index entry for "England, Births and Christenings, 1538-1975" (ark:/61903/1:1:NX2H-W9B) records the christening of Robert Swarbrick on 20 December 1797 at Poulton le Fylde, Lancashire, England, naming John Swarbrick as father and Grace (no surname in index) as mother.¹ This is a derivative source — a FamilySearch database compiled by the Genealogical Society of Utah from parish register microfilm — but the information is primary (recorded by the officiating minister at the time of the event) and the evidence is direct (it names the child, date, place, and both parents). The original register image is accessible at the cited URL but was not examined in this research.

### Sibling-Cluster Corroboration

A second search targeting all children of John Swarbrick and Grace in Lancashire christening records (log_003) independently returned nine entries: Alice (8 March 1789), Margaret (6 March 1791), Betty (17 February 1793), Jane (9 June 1795), Robert (20 December 1797), and John (20 December 1797, twin of Robert), all christened at Poulton-le-Fylde.² The twin John's entry (ark:/61903/1:1:NX2H-WS9) is derivative/primary/direct by the same classification as Robert's, and derives from the same register event; it therefore represents one information unit jointly with NX2H-W9B rather than an independent second unit. The cluster as a whole, however, was derived by a separate search strategy (searching for the parents' children, not for Robert directly), and its nine entries spanning 1789–1797 independently establish John Swarbrick + Grace as a Poulton-le-Fylde family active throughout the period. This corroborates that the 20 December 1797 Robert in NX2H-W9B is a child of this couple.

### Biographical and Geographic Consistency

Robert Swarbrick (MM4W-19N) married Ellen Gardner on 20 February 1821 at Poulton-le-Fylde and died at Hambleton (an adjacent township) circa 20 November 1839, being buried 22 November 1839 at St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde. His entire recorded life is anchored at Poulton-le-Fylde and the immediately surrounding area. His father John Swarbrick (L88T-VT4) was buried at St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde on 7 August 1830, and his mother Grace (P6MH-1N8, née Collinson) on 25 April 1825 — placing both parents, throughout their lives, at the same parish. This geographic coherence strongly supports the Poulton-le-Fylde christening as Robert's.

### Exclusion of the Competing Entry

A separate FamilySearch index entry (ark:/61903/1:1:V5GJ-FJG) records the christening of a Robert Swarbrick on 11 June 1797 at Hambleton, Lancashire, with father William Swarbrick and mother Ann.³ This entry names a demonstrably different father (William, not John) and mother (Ann, not Grace). The given names of both parents differ completely from MM4W-19N's family; the parish (Hambleton) differs from the Poulton-le-Fylde cluster. There is no evidence connecting MM4W-19N to a William Swarbrick or an Ann. This entry is treated as negative evidence: its existence as a same-name same-year record does not challenge the Poulton-le-Fylde identification — it confirms only that a different Robert Swarbrick existed nearby.

## Source Limitations

All three sources consulted (src_001–src_003) are derivative FamilySearch indexes; the original St. Chad, Poulton-le-Fylde parish register images and the Hambleton register images were not examined. The indexes are products of the GSU's systematic microfilming program and are internally consistent. The sibling cluster across nine separate register entries provides corroboration that would require multiple simultaneous transcription errors to overturn. Nonetheless, a transcription error in the index that changed a parent's name cannot be excluded without examining the original images. The original Poulton-le-Fylde register image for NX2H-W9B is accessible at FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-D49Q-373?cc=1473014); examining it would advance the conclusion toward the proved tier.

## Tier

**Probable.** The preponderance of available evidence strongly supports the conclusion that Robert Swarbrick (MM4W-19N) was christened 20 December 1797 at Poulton-le-Fylde, Lancashire, as the son of John Swarbrick (L88T-VT4) and Grace Collinson (P6MH-1N8). The competing Hambleton entry is excluded by its different parentage. The conclusion falls short of proved because the primary sources are derivative indexes rather than examined originals.

---

### Citations

¹ "England, Births and Christenings, 1538-1975," database, FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:9WMS-KWK : 5 May 2026), Entry for Robert Swarbrick, 20 Dec 1797, Poulton le Fylde, Lancashire; citing index based upon data collected by the Genealogical Society of Utah, Salt Lake City. (src_002)

² "England, Births and Christenings, 1538-1975," database, FamilySearch, search for children of John Swarbrick and Grace, Lancashire 1785-1815 (log_003); nine results at Poulton-le-Fylde, including Robert (20 Dec 1797, ark:/61903/1:1:NX2H-W9B) and twin John (20 Dec 1797, ark:/61903/1:1:NX2H-WS9). (src_001)

³ "England, Births and Christenings, 1538-1975," database, FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:9MHY-8S3G : 5 February 2023), entry for Robert Swarbrick, 11 Jun 1797, Hambleton, Lancashire, father William Swarbrick, mother Ann. (src_003)
